result.markdown: --- title: "How does Nokia 3210 Works?" subtitle: "Networks, Radio, Embedded Systems" description: "A teardown of the Nokia 3210, exploring its hardware components, network connectivity, and embedded systems architecture." author: "Rick Rejeleene" date: 2026-05-20 date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY" filters: - d2 categories: - Reverse Engineering - Engineering - Tutorial - Electrical Engineering - Electronics - Embedded Systems - Computer Science format: html: css: styles.css toc: true toc-title: "Contents" toc-depth: 2 --- ## Introduction In this post, We will be taking a deep dive into the inner workings of the Nokia 3210 which had a 25th anniversary, launched on May 8, 2024. The reason why we are going to do a teardown of the Nokia 3210 is to understand how it works, and to learn about the hardware components, network connectivity, and embedded systems architecture that make it function. ```{html} ``` In case, if you had not a chance to read basics of How a Mobile Phone works? You can read it here: [How does a Mobile Phone Works?](https://rickrejeleene.me/Tamil/posts/2026-05-13NokiaPhone/index.html) >Few highlights and reflections from the previous posts. One of the most interesting things for me was realizing that our voice calls are travelling through complex networks and it travels at light speed, which is really fascinating. Our voice is converted into digital signals, and goes through many complex infrastructures, and it reaches the other person in a matter of milliseconds. One fundamental research question you could ponder for future is: **We are able to translate information into light speed, but why not humans could travel at light speed?** ## Teardown of Nokia 3210 In order to do a teardown, make sure you have the tools required for the teardown, such as a screwdriver, tweezers. I got myself a Hyper Tough 28 Piece Precision Screwdriver Set, which is really good for this kind of work. ```{=html} ``` For organizing the screw and components, Magnetic Parts Holder is really useful, it helps to keep the screws and components organized and prevents them from getting lost. A hyper touch precision screwdriver with size T5H that has magnetic tip is really useful for opening the screws of the Nokia 3210. The first step in the teardown process is to remove the back cover of the phone. This can be done by using a screwdriver to remove the screws that hold the back cover in place. Once the screws are removed, you can gently pry open the back cover using a plastic opening tool or your fingers.
